steps.title: Translate Function Definition to Equation
steps.content: The function is defined as $ f(x) = 4x + 19 $. The $ x $-intercept occurs when $ f(x) = 0 $, which corresponds to solving $ 0 = 4x + 19 $. This is a linear equation where $ x $ must be isolated directly.

steps.title: Solve for the x-Intercept
steps.content: Subtract 19 from both sides: $ -19 = 4x $. Divide by 4: $ x = -\frac{19}{4} $. This value represents the point where the graph crosses the $ x $-axis (when $ y = 0 $).

steps.title: Match to the Correct Interpreation
steps.content:
Choice A explicitly states $ x = -\frac{19}{4} $ when $ f(x) = 0 $.

This matches the derived solution and directly describes the x-intercept.

Other choices refer to $ f(0) $ (y-intercept) or slope behavior, which are irrelevant to the x-intercept definition.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
